title: 1981-04-16_Pat_130.txt
key_claims: Reviewing his exegesis, Dick realizes his theology of dread, sickness unto death, choice, and absolute faith aligns perfectly with Søren Kierkegaard, the father of existential thought.
True Christianity is secretly an ecstatic religion; the 2-3-74 event transmuted Dick's dread and sorrow into profound joy, overthrowing the "King of Tears."
Dick outlines an "8-volume meta-novel" (including *Confessions of a Crap Artist*, *Androids*, *Tears*, *Scanner*, *VALIS*, and *Divine Invasion*). The central arc is the evolution of the "Fool."

The Fool evolves from a mundane idiot (Jack Isidore), to a Holy Fool protecting animals (J.R. Isidore), to the Holy Fool seized and possessed by Christ (Bill Lundborg). *VALIS* then explains the mechanics of this divine seizure via Horselover Fat.
recurring_concepts: **The Ravished Fool**: Borrowing from John Donne's sonnet ("unless you ravish mee"), Dick explains that the Holy Fool does not *become* Christ; he is forcefully invaded and utterly consumed by the Holy Spirit.
reading_excerpt: Reviewing his exegesis, Dick realizes his theology of dread, sickness unto death, choice, and absolute faith aligns perfectly with Søren Kierkegaard, the father of existential thought.
